name: Tablillas Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalse de Tablillas desde Hinojosas de Calatrava — circular
description: This difficult 23.8-mile touring cycling route from Hinojosas de Calatrava explores the Embalse de Tablillas and local heritage.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Hinojosas De Calatrava offers diverse landscapes within the Natural Park of Valle de Alcudia and Sierra Madrona. The region features a mix of vast plains, rugged mountains, and wooded pastures known as dehesas. Cyclists can explore areas characterized by oak groves, a unique volcanic landscape with agricultural fields, and scenic reservoirs. Best touring cycling routes around Hinojosas De Calatrava The most popular touring cycling route is Montoro and Tablillas Reservoirs Loop, a 27.3 miles (44.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This route explores the scenic Montoro and Tablillas reservoirs, offering varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Fuencaliente and Conquista Loop, a difficult 37.7 miles (60.6 km) path. This route traverses the natural park, providing views of the region's diverse flora and fauna. Local touring cyclists also love the Climb to the Castle Convent of Calatrava La Nueva — circular from Puertollano, a 44.9 miles (72.2 km) trail leading through historical sites and elevated terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 25 minutes. Touring cycling around Hinojosas De Calatrava is defined by the Valle de Alcudia and Sierra Madrona Natural Park, volcanic landscapes, and significant elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Hinojosas De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 18 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Hinojosas De Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Hinojosas de Calatrava?
A: There are over 60 touring cycling routes around Hinojosas de Calatrava, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find routes ranging from moderate to difficult, with significant elevation changes in some areas.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Hinojosas de Calatrava?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from vast plains to rugged mountains within the Natural Park of Valle de Alcudia and Sierra Madrona. You'll cycle through oak groves (encinares), dehesas (wooded pastures), and unique volcanic landscapes with agricultural fields, vineyards, and olive groves. Some routes also feature scenic reservoirs.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in the area?
A: While many routes in Hinojosas de Calatrava are rated moderate to difficult due to elevation changes, there are 6 routes classified as easy. These typically offer less challenging terrain, making them suitable for families or those seeking a more relaxed ride. Look for routes with lower elevation gains for a more family-friendly experience.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while cycling?
A: Cyclists can explore the expansive Valle de Alcudia and Sierra Madrona Natural Park, offering panoramic views. Routes often pass by water bodies like the Embalse de Tablillas and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5632567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano del Montoro&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter historical sites like the Castillete de la Mina de la Simona, a reminder of the region's mining past, or ancient rock paintings at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3169663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaescrita Cave Paintings&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Hinojosas de Calatrava?
A: The spring and autumn months are ideal for touring cycling in Hinojosas de Calatrava. During these seasons, temperatures are moderate, making for more comfortable rides. The landscapes are also particularly beautiful, with lush greenery in spring and vibrant colors in autumn.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Hinojosas de Calatrava are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058415241/montoro-and-tablillas-reservoirs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montoro and Tablillas Reservoirs Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056924351/fuencaliente-and-conquista-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuencaliente and Conquista Loop&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options that allow you to start and end in the same location.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Hinojosas de Calatrava?
A: The touring cycling routes in Hinojosas de Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from 18 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the challenge of the varied terrain, and the opportunity to explore both natural parks and historical sites.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or water bodies?
A: Absolutely. The region features several reservoirs that are focal points for cycling routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058415241/montoro-and-tablillas-reservoirs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montoro and Tablillas Reservoirs Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, exploring the scenic Montoro and Tablillas reservoirs and offering beautiful waterside views.

Q: Can I find routes that include historical sites or cultural points of interest?
A: Yes, touring cycling in Hinojosas de Calatrava can be enriched with cultural and historical stops. The town itself has an enchanting medieval character. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052875068/climb-to-the-castle-convent-of-calatrava-la-nueva-circular-from-puertollano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to the Castle Convent of Calatrava La Nueva — circular from Puertollano&lt;/a&gt; lead through historical sites and elevated terrain, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for advanced touring cyclists?
A: Yes, Hinojosas de Calatrava offers 31 routes classified as difficult, catering to advanced touring cyclists. These routes often feature significant elevation gains and cover longer distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374615022/hinojosas-de-calatrava-and-almod" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hinojosas de Calatrava and Almod&lt;/a&gt; route, providing a challenging and rewarding experience.

Q: What are the options for parking when cycling in Hinojosas de Calatrava?
A: While specific parking facilities for cyclists are not detailed, Hinojosas de Calatrava is a small town with a medieval urban fabric. You can typically find parking within or near the village center, which serves as a convenient starting point for many routes into the surrounding natural park.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or unique geological features to see?
A: Yes, the region boasts natural attractions such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4342897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batanera Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the area is known for its unique volcanic landscape, including features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6295627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maar and Strombolian volcano of Almodóvar del Campo&lt;/a&gt;, which add geological interest to your cycling tours.
tips: Municipality that belongs to the Valle de Alcudia Region, as an independent municipality since 1848 when it was separated from Puertollano, but there is evidence of the existence of a population center since at least the 16th century. Passing through Hinojosas, it stops next to the parish church of San Bernardo where there is a fountain.
The Tablillas reservoir is located on the river of the same name, it is really one of the tail reservoirs of the Montoro reservoir. It is a sanitary swamp built in 1950.

At the entrance to the town Cabezarrubias del Puerto we can see this small stone construction with a broom (straw) roof. In the past, shepherds and farmers used this type of shelter to protect themselves from rain and cold.
Nearby is also an old laundry room.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Embalse de Tablillas from Hinojosas de Calatrava — circular route?
faqs.answer: This touring cycling route is classified as difficult. It requires very good physical fitness, and you might encounter sections where pushing your bicycle is necessary due to the demanding terrain. The route involves over 500 meters of elevation gain and typically takes more than five hours to complete.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a varied landscape. You'll cycle approximately 19.2 kilometers on roads (carretera), 18.6 kilometers on paths or trails (senda), and 414 meters on streets (calle). Specifically, about 18.1 kilometers are on unpaved track, and there are roughly 10.0 kilometers each of asphalt and paved track.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or sights can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several interesting points. The prominent feature is the Tablillas Reservoir itself. In Hinojosas de Calatrava, you can see the parish church of San Bernardo and a fountain. Further along, near Cabezarrubias del Puerto, look out for a traditional shepherd's hut (chozo) and an old washing place, offering glimpses into local heritage.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging route typically takes around 3 hours and 6 minutes of active cycling time. However, given its 'difficult' classification and varied terrain, plan for a total duration of more than five hours, including stops.

faqs.question: Where does the Embalse de Tablillas from Hinojosas de Calatrava — circular route start, and is there parking?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Hinojosas de Calatrava. As it's a circular route from the settlement, you should be able to find parking within the village itself.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and is not suitable for beginners. It requires very good physical fitness and experience with varied terrain, including unpaved sections and significant elevation changes.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de Tablillas from Hinojosas de Calatrava — circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas, it is primarily designed for touring cycling and includes road sections. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it is well-behaved, accustomed to cycling environments, and kept on a leash, especially near roads and in populated areas. Always carry water for your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given its location in Ciudad Real, Spain, spring and autumn are generally the best times to cycle this route. The temperatures are milder, making the significant distance and elevation gain more manageable. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring colder, wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route or the Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this touring cycling route or the general areas of the Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona Natural Park through which it passes. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several local paths. You will encounter sections of the Cañada de Puerto Suelta, camino plaza de toros, camino alto de semda de la calera, camino cementerio municipal, Calle de la biblioteca, and Camino de la Pocita.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or pubs near the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Hinojosas de Calatrava, where you may find some local establishments for refreshments or a meal. However, facilities are sparse along the majority of the route, so it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks.
